response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with a long track record, but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. He lacks native-level Japanese (a hard requirement), has no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation/modification experience, and shows no background in embedded networking, LAN-switch products, or security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or tamper detection. His C experience is limited to a brief ~7-month stint over a decade ago; his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang web systems.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — the role requires native-level Japanese
No Linux kernel, embedded networking, network security (MACsec, 802.1X), or switch-product experience
C for UNIX proficiency is required, but recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js with only brief, dated C/C++ exposure

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Solid general engineering seniority and low-latency systems background
